# Fixtures — what each one proves

All content is **synthetic** (no real transcripts, no secrets). Every fixture is a valid
Windowpane input; M1 tests must encode the assertions below. Assertions are structural —
absolute token numbers come from `js-tiktoken` at runtime and must not be hardcoded, with one
exception: `totalTokens` is always exact (from the fixture's `usage` values).

Universal invariant, asserted for **every** fixture:
`sum(snapshot.buckets[].tokens) === snapshot.totalTokens` (exactly), and no bucket is negative.

## `basic.jsonl` — happy path + requestId dedup

- 3 assistant records share `req_001` with identical usage; 1 record has `req_002`.
- Expect: `totalTokens === 9120` (from `req_002`: 120+1000+8000), **not** any sum over records.
- `turns.length === 2`; turn[0].windowTokens === 5000, turn[1].windowTokens === 9120.
- `model === "claude-opus-4-8"`, `windowLimit === 1_000_000`.
- Buckets present: `file-reads` (1 item, label contains `config.json`), `thinking`,
  `assistant-text`, `user-messages`, `tool-calls`, `system-overhead` (> 0).
- The `mode` record is kind `meta` — contributes nothing.
- `cacheEfficiency ≈ 8000/9120` (last turn).

## `no-usage.jsonl` — session with no assistant turn yet

- Expect: `isEmpty === true`, `totalTokens === 0`, `turns.length === 0`,
  every bucket zero/empty, `prune` empty, no throw.

## `multi-mcp.jsonl` — MCP server grouping + image + array tool_result

- Expect buckets `mcp:ado` and `mcp:figma`, each with exactly 1 item.
- The figma tool_result has array content (text + image): image contributes the flat image
  estimate into the **same** `mcp:figma` item (attribution follows the tool, not block type,
  for tool_result inner blocks).
- Parallel tool_use: two tool_use blocks in one assistant record resolve independently.
- `model === "claude-fable-5"` → `windowLimit === 1_000_000`.

## `compaction.jsonl` — compact boundary cutoff

- Expect: no item whose label/text derives from a record before the `compact_boundary`
  (grep snapshot items for `PRE-BOUNDARY` → none).
- The `isCompactSummary` record lands in `injected-context`.
- `POST-BOUNDARY` user/assistant text is attributed normally.
- `totalTokens === 31_650` (from `req_202`: 150+1500+30000); `turns` includes pre-boundary
  turns (timeline shows history) but `buckets/items` only post-boundary content.

## `unknown-types.jsonl` — tolerance

- `flux-capacitor` record and the invalid JSON line → `unknown` bucket, tokens ≈ byteLength/4
  each, **no throw**.
- `bridge-session` is a *known* meta type → kind `meta`, NOT in `unknown`.
- `isMeta` user record with `<system-reminder>` → `injected-context`, not `user-messages`.
- Unknown block type `hologram` inside a known assistant record → the `unknown` bucket,
  estimated as JSON-stringified length / 4 (the sibling `text` block still lands in
  `assistant-text` normally).
- `model === "experimental-model-x"` → `windowLimit === null` (no gauge percentage).

## `redundant-reads.jsonl` — prune heuristics (generated, ~52KB)

- `/tmp/proj/notes.md` read 3× → one `redundant-read` suggestion, `itemIds.length === 3`,
  `reclaimableTokens === est(read1) + est(read2)` (all but the newest).
- One Bash tool_result of ~48K chars → estimated ≳ 10K tokens → one `large-payload` suggestion.
- Suggestions sorted by `reclaimableTokens` descending.
- `totalTokens === 22_100` (from `req_411`: 100+13000+9000). Note the attributed estimate
  (~12K+ tokens of payloads) stays below the exact total → positive overhead; if an estimator
  change ever pushes attributed above total, the proportional-scaling rule from
  docs/contracts.md applies — assert the invariant, not the overhead value.

## Regenerating `redundant-reads.jsonl`

Generated by a small node script (see git history of this directory) so the 48K payload never
has to live in an editor buffer. If you change prune thresholds (docs/decisions.md D14),
regenerate proportionally.
